title: N.H. police chief placed on leave after video released of him grabbing someone by the throat inside police station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gorham, NH Police Chief Jimmy Willhoite is on leave after video shows him grabbing a man by the throat.
The altercation involved Stephen Gregory, who was reporting a threat at the police station when the incident occurred.
New Hampshire's Department of Justice Public Integrity Unit is investigating the incident along with the NH Police Standards and Training Council.

title: Man, 29, killed in daytime shooting in Dorchester
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 29-year-old man was fatally shot in Dorchester on Wednesday morning, according to Boston police.
Officers responded to 15 York St., where the victim was found and pronounced dead by Boston EMS.
No arrests have been made, and police urge anyone with information to contact the Boston Police CrimeStoppers Program.

title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston men arrested for Mattapan shooting that left one dead and four injured on June 27, 2026.
Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris face multiple charges including assault and armed assault to murder.
Both suspects pleaded not guilty and are held at Suffolk County Jail; court dates set for August 4.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reports 18 cases of cyclosporiasis between May 1 and July 1, consistent with normal seasonal levels.
The Midwestern U.S. faces a cyclospora outbreak with over 400 infections across Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and West Virginia.
Cyclospora, often linked to fresh produce, spreads via contaminated food or water and is treatable with antibiotics.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 featured the Parade of Sail event, showcasing tall ships in Boston Harbor on July 11, 2025.
Courageous Sailing Center adapted its youth programs by moving activities upriver to avoid crowds from docking tall ships.
Community Boating, Inc. used the event to promote accessibility in sailing, engaging with visitors and continuing its programs.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts issued an air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke through 12 a.m. Thursday.
The alert highlights elevated PM2.5 levels, posing risks to sensitive groups including the elderly and children.
MassDEP advises sensitive groups to limit outdoor activity; Boston's air quality deemed unhealthy for these groups.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The historic Dutch tall ship, Oosterschelde, is docked at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026.
Oosterschelde offers public tours daily from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m., showcasing its historical features above and below deck.
Sail Boston 2026 is part of Sail250, featuring over 50 ships from more than 20 countries to celebrate America's 250th birthday.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer, a rising music artist, found Bob Dylan in her dressing room after performing at Outlaw Music Fest.
Bob Dylan requested Brittney Spencer to join his tour two years after their unusual meeting during a concert.
Spencer will open for Dylan alongside Jimmie Vaughan at Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 16.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: OpenTable's 2026 list includes five top hotel restaurants in New England, located in Massachusetts, Maine, and Connecticut.
Boston's Contessa and Mooo Beacon Hill, and Kennebunkport's Earth at Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn are recognized.
Michael Jordan’s Steak House at Mohegan Sun, Uncasville, Connecticut, is also among the top 100 hotel restaurants.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović, New England Revolution head coach, reminisces about his childhood World Cup memories growing up in Belgrade.
Mitrović recalls watching the 1982 World Cup as a catalyst for his love of soccer, despite not remembering specific games.
Although Mitrović never played in a senior World Cup, he found pride coaching Serbia to a 2015 FIFA U20 World Cup victory.
